title: Project Management
summary: A provider-agnostic kanban toolkit: the same board workflow runs locally (board.json), on Trello, or on Jira — auto-detected, no account required. Covers the full card lifecycle from intake to shipped: triage and size, refine, break down, prune dead weight, map dependencies, and ship with proof.
problem: Board management is tool-locked and inconsistent — work lacks sizing, dependencies are invisible, backlogs rot, and cards get marked done without proof. This standardizes the workflow whether or not you have an online board.

skills.name: ship
skills.purpose: Take the top card from claim to "In review": verify it isn't already done, build + test, commit, move it, keep the board in sync — never on an unproven claim.

skills.name: triage
skills.purpose: Sort an unsorted pile: route, prioritize, size (absolute OR by comparison to 2 reference cards), detect dependencies (writes depends-on:), flag dupes/oversized/blocked.

skills.name: refine
skills.purpose: Backlog refinement / groom / enrich: bring thin cards to "ready" — context, acceptance criteria, estimate, priority — and health-pass the backlog top.

skills.name: breakdown
skills.purpose: Split an oversized or vague card into 3–7 small, ordered, independently-shippable subtasks with acceptance criteria.

skills.name: prune
skills.purpose: Remove dead weight — duplicates, done-but-open, obsolete, zombie cards — proposing removals with reasons and confirming before deleting.

skills.name: dependency-graph
skills.purpose: Print the dependency graph of open + recently-closed cards — Mermaid + ASCII tree + cycle/dangling diagnostics — from the depends-on: lines triage writes.
